meta.title: ORKM-313 Remaining Review Findings
meta.description: Detailed technical explainer for the two review findings left after the schedule-submission fix

props.title: Two Findings Still Matter
props.subtitle: The schedule-submission bug is fixed. These are the remaining regressions that still need code changes before the broader patch is safe.

props.content: Both remaining issues are state-correctness problems, not polish. One can silently degrade real OMA users into the wrong no-appointment branch after a transient failure. The other turns malformed cached session data into an authenticated-flow fallback to anonymous mode.

rows.trigger: One token/API/network failure while loading services for the selected OMA account/site
rows.observed: UI settles into the no-upcoming-service branch and does not retry for that same selection
rows.why: The request key is cached before the async call and never cleared in the error path
rows.blast: Wrong scheduling/confirmation variant until reload or context switch

rows.trigger: Corrupt or partial sessionStorage payload for the OMA cache key
rows.observed: Authenticated OMA bootstrap falls through to anonymous quote mode
rows.why: New debug logging parses cached JSON before the guarded cache fallback block
rows.blast: User loses OMA bootstrap path instead of refetching fresh account data

props.title: Why this is a real regression
props.content: The new appointment state directly controls whether OMA users see a real upcoming-service experience or a fallback 'branch will call' experience. Because the fetch key is marked as already loaded before the request completes, the same account/site pair will not retry automatically after a transient failure.

items.title: Selection resolves
items.description: `useOMAQuoteState` builds `fetchKey = accountId:siteId` and writes it into `appointmentFetchKeyRef.current` before the network call starts.

items.title: Request fails once
items.description: A token miss, backend error, or network blip drops into the catch path.

items.title: State is downgraded
items.description: The code sets `omaNextAppointment = null` and `omaHasExistingAppointment = false`, which selects the fallback branch.

items.title: No automatic retry
items.description: Because the same fetch key remains cached, later renders short-circuit instead of retrying the request for that account/site pair.

rows.detail: The fetch key is written before the request finishes.
The error path clears appointment state but does not clear the fetch key.
The query window was also narrowed to one month, which increases the chance of false negatives even when the call succeeds.

props.title: The logging changed behavior
props.content: Before this patch, malformed cached OMA data could fall through the guarded parse block and refetch from the accounts API. After the patch, the new debug log parses the cache first. If that parse throws, control jumps to the outer catch and the hook downgrades the user to the anonymous flow.

rows.before: Guarded parse failed and the hook could continue toward a fresh API read
rows.after: Logging parse can throw before the guarded block even runs
Now also captures cache-shape problems introduced by the debug parse
Anonymous fallback with lost OMA context

props.title: 1. Repair retry semantics
props.content: Clear `appointmentFetchKeyRef.current` on failure or switch to a separate success marker. That keeps transient errors from freezing the wrong branch.

props.title: 2. Move debug parsing inside the safe block
props.content: The fix is mechanical: do not `JSON.parse(cached)` until the existing guarded cache block. If the parse fails, refetch instead of degrading to anonymous.

props.title: 3. Verify with two throwaway harnesses
props.content: Run one transient repro where the appointment-services call fails once for a stable account/site, and one where sessionStorage holds malformed cached JSON. Both should recover without selecting the wrong user branch.
